candidates.name: Forward-Future/DeployBot
candidates.title: DeployBot
candidates.description: Agent-managed GitHub merge queue for Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, and MCP clients.
candidates.signal_summary: New MCP server (created 2026-06-20, 38 stars) that manages GitHub merge queues for AI coding agents. Pushed actively on 2026-06-22. MIT licensed, Python.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Addresses a real orchestration gap: coordinating merge/PR workflows across multiple AI coding agents (Codex, Claude Code, Cursor) without human bottleneck.

candidates.name: pranjalbhatia710/descartes-mcp
candidates.title: Descartes MCP
candidates.description: An MCP server that recursively doubts every plan decision — grills agent reasoning before execution.
candidates.signal_summary: New MCP server (created 2026-06-21, 22 stars) implementing adversarial reasoning as a tool. MIT licensed, Python.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Novel safety/reliability pattern: an MCP tool that stress-tests agent plans before execution. Could reduce hallucination-driven failures.

candidates.name: vornicx/Midas
candidates.title: Midas
candidates.description: Local-first, eval-first memory for long-horizon AI agents — no LLM at ingest. Python SDK + MCP server.
candidates.signal_summary: Memory system for long-horizon agents (11 stars, created 2026-06-04). Apache-2.0, Python. Pushed 2026-06-22. Emphasizes eval-first and local-first design.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Addresses a key agent reliability problem: memory quality for long-running tasks. The 'no LLM at ingest' design reduces cost and hallucination in memory formation.

candidates.name: aryaminus/controlkeel
candidates.title: ControlKeel
candidates.description: Agent control plane for governed AI coding: validate changes, enforce policy gates, track findings, and audit agent actions.
candidates.signal_summary: Agent governance/control plane (8 stars, created 2026-03-18). Elixir. Pushed 2026-06-20. License unclear (NOASSERTION).
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Policy enforcement layer for AI coding agents — validates changes before they land. Relevant for teams deploying agents in regulated or high-stakes environments.

candidates.name: natea/harness-eval
candidates.title: Harness Eval
candidates.description: Eval framework that ranks agentic coding frameworks (Superpowers, Compound Engineering, Agent Skills) head-to-head.
candidates.signal_summary: Eval framework for comparing agentic coding frameworks (6 stars, created 2026-06-10). TypeScript. Pushed 2026-06-21. License not asserted.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Head-to-head comparison data for agent frameworks helps builders make informed stack choices. Fills a gap in the ecosystem.

candidates.name: TerryCM/vibegroup
candidates.title: VibeGroup
candidates.description: Let your team's Claude Code agents talk to each other, end-to-end: ask what a teammate is building, share context, coordinate work.
candidates.signal_summary: Multi-agent coordination tool (7 stars, created 2026-06-21). TypeScript, MIT. Pushed 2026-06-22. Enables Claude Code agents to share context and coordinate.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Multi-agent collaboration is a growing pain point. This gives Claude Code agents a shared communication channel without custom orchestration.

candidates.name: timo-reymann/mealie-mcp-server
candidates.title: Mealie MCP Server
candidates.description: MCP server for Mealie recipe management. Exposes tools and prompts for recipe search, meal planning, and ingredient management.
candidates.signal_summary: Niche MCP server for Mealie recipe manager (4 stars, created 2026-06-20). TypeScript, MIT. Shows MCP ecosystem expanding into consumer/home-use domains.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Illustrates MCP's expansion beyond dev tools into consumer applications. Low risk, well-scoped.

candidates.name: mcp-fit
candidates.title: mcp-fit
candidates.description: MCP server agent-usability scorer — scores and auto-fixes tool descriptions for better agent comprehension.
candidates.signal_summary: npm package (v0.1.1, score 157.8) that scores and auto-fixes MCP tool descriptions for agent usability. Addresses a real quality problem in the MCP ecosystem.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Poor tool descriptions are a common MCP pain point. This package could improve agent reliability across any MCP server by optimizing descriptions.

candidates.name: @gork-labs/secondbrain-mcp
candidates.title: Second Brain MCP
candidates.description: Second Brain MCP Server - Agent team orchestration with dynamic tool discovery.
candidates.signal_summary: npm package (v0.23.0, score 148.1) for agent team orchestration with dynamic tool discovery. Higher version number suggests active development.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Dynamic tool discovery is a key pattern for multi-agent systems. This package could simplify orchestration setup.

candidates.name: @openhorizon/workflows
candidates.title: OpenHorizon Workflows
candidates.description: Deterministic multi-agent workflow orchestration as an MCP server — agent(), parallel(), pipeline() primitives.
candidates.signal_summary: npm package (v0.1.5, score 141.0) providing deterministic multi-agent workflow primitives (agent/parallel/pipeline) as MCP tools.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Deterministic workflow orchestration is a key need for production agent systems. Having these primitives as MCP tools is a clean design.

errors: GitHub API rate limit exhausted during scout — some repo detail fetches returned null. Core data from search results is intact.
